title: Advertising preclearance - Canada.ca
content:
## Advertising preclearance
Health Canada is committed to protecting the health and safety of Canadians by establishing regulatory standards for health product advertising. Preclearance of advertising material by arms-length agencies remains voluntary for industry, but Health Canada strongly recommends it. Industry is responsible for meeting federally legislated advertising requirements.
Recommended criteria for the preclearance of advertising material of non-prescription drugs and natural health products directed to consumers
Only agencies that have notified Health Canada of their public attestation to meeting Health Canada's criteria are included. Although Health Canada collaborates with these agencies, this list does not constitute recognition or endorsement. It is provided as a resource for consumers and industry to make informed choices when selecting a preclearance agency for advertising review.
Health Canada is the national regulatory authority for health product advertising. Advertisements must not be false, misleading or deceptive. To support this, Health Canada provides policies to effectively regulate marketed health products, issues guidelines for interpreting the Regulations and oversees regulated advertising activities.
Health Canada applies a risk-based approach to compliance and enforcement activities and reserves the right to enforce the advertising provisions under federal legislation.

## List of Canadian advertising preclearance agencies
